meaning: adj.和气的,有民间风味的,友好的,无拘束的
examples: They wanted the store to have a folksy small-town image.
Huckabee, a former governor of Arkansas who mixes folksy charm with bedrock Christian conservative views, likens his rise to the aeronautics of the bumblebee.
His speeches are folksy and witty.
Certainly in style: Thompson is folksy, genial and he knows how to work a camera.
Lay will put on a folksy show, maybe using a bit more twang than colleagues remember.
